test_series_name: Customs in International Context - 12 Main Tests
notes: These tests are based on the uploaded Customs in International Context presentation.
The sequence follows a progression from foundational recall to broader application and mixed-topic reasoning.
Time limits increase toward later tests.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid consecutive repetition of the same answer letter.

tests.title: Customs in International Context - International Trade - Main Test 1
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the presentation.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a road consignment note?

options.A: A sea freight title document
options.B: A road transport document proving receipt, evidencing contract of carriage, invoicing freight, and guiding handling
options.C: A customs valuation certificate
options.D: An importer’s tax return

questions.explanation: The road consignment note serves several transport functions.

questions.question: What are insurance documents?

options.A: Documents proving trade balance surplus
options.B: Documents used to issue HS Codes
options.C: Documents authorizing customs duty remission
options.D: Documents protecting against loss or damage during transit, such as an insurance certificate or policy

questions.explanation: Insurance documents cover transit risk.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is entrepôt or re-export trade?

options.A: Importing goods and then re-exporting them to another country without major processing
options.B: Importing goods for domestic consumption only
options.C: Exporting domestically produced goods only
options.D: Transporting goods only within one country

questions.explanation: The presentation defines re-export trade in this way.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the five main categories of international trade documents?

options.A: Domestic, tax, customs, labor, and judicial documents
options.B: Export, import, re-export, bilateral, and multilateral documents
options.C: Commercial, transport, financial, insurance, and official/regulatory documents
options.D: Sales, carrier, warehouse, banking, and diplomatic documents

questions.explanation: The presentation groups trade documents into these five categories.

questions.question: What is a delivery order?

options.A: A document issued by the carrier or agent enabling the consignee or forwarding agent to take delivery of cargo
options.B: A customs tariff decision
options.C: A purchase order amendment
options.D: A bank loan approval note

questions.explanation: Delivery orders facilitate collection from the vessel or port.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which documents may be used at the order stage?

options.A: Certificate of origin and delivery order only
options.B: Insurance policy and cargo receipt only
options.C: Requisition, purchase order, telex, fax, and proforma invoice
options.D: Bill of lading, airway bill, and manifest

questions.explanation: These are order-stage documents.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one key Incoterms 2020 change regarding DAT?

options.A: DAT became a payment method
options.B: DAT was renamed DPU to show delivery unloaded can occur at any place, not only a terminal
options.C: DAT was abolished with no replacement
options.D: DAT was merged into FOB

questions.explanation: DAT became DPU in Incoterms 2020.

questions.question: Why is air transport often chosen?

options.A: Because it automatically transfers title
options.B: Because it is the only option for global trade
options.C: Because it is always the cheapest option
options.D: Because it is fast and suitable for urgent, perishable, or high-value goods, though expensive

questions.explanation: Air transport trades cost for speed.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is customs clearance in international trade?

options.A: It means only opening a letter of credit
options.B: It means only calculating trade balance
options.C: It includes not only payment of duties and taxes but also compliance with administrative requirements
options.D: It means only paying the carrier freight

questions.explanation: Clearance is broader than tax payment alone.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does FCA mean?

options.A: Free Carrier means the seller delivers the goods, cleared for export, to the carrier nominated by the buyer at the named place
options.B: Seller retains all risks until final buyer delivery
options.C: Seller delivers only after import clearance
options.D: Seller must unload goods at destination

questions.explanation: FCA requires delivery to the buyer’s nominated carrier.

questions.question: When was the Kenya–EU EPA signed and when did it come into force?

options.A: Signed in December 2020 and in force from January 2021
options.B: Signed on 18 December 2023 and came into force on 1 July 2024
options.C: Signed in 2024 and in force in 2025
options.D: Signed in 2013 and in force in 2015

questions.explanation: These are the dates given in the presentation.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Kenya reduces tariffs gradually on some EU goods, keeps some lines excluded, but obtains duty-free access for its goods into the EU. Which agreement framework is this?

options.A: The Kenya–UK EPA alone
options.B: A documentary collection arrangement
options.C: Incoterms 2020
options.D: The Kenya–EU EPA

questions.explanation: This matches the Kenya–EU EPA structure described in the presentation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a tariff?

options.A: A freight charge by the carrier
options.B: A tax on imports or exports
options.C: An insurance premium
options.D: A tax on personal income only

questions.explanation: The presentation defines tariff simply as a tax on imports or exports.

questions.question: Why is maritime transportation important to globalization?

options.A: Because it is relevant only to local trade
options.B: Because it eliminates the need for inland transport
options.C: Because it enables movement of goods between continents and supports the global supply chain
options.D: Because it replaces all other modes

questions.explanation: Sea transport underpins global cargo movement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is confirmation in international trade?

options.A: The bank's refusal to finance the transaction
options.B: The customs release of cargo
options.C: The consignee's transport booking
options.D: A document from the seller to the buyer confirming the terms of delivery

questions.explanation: Confirmation records the seller's acceptance or confirmation of terms.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.B: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.C: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied
options.D: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: What is a letter of credit (LC)?

options.A: A title document for sea carriage
options.B: A road transport receipt only
options.C: A tax exemption certificate
options.D: A bank commitment on behalf of the buyer that payment will be made if stated terms and required documents are satisfied

questions.explanation: An LC is a conditional bank undertaking.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What labor and ethical concerns can arise from international trade?

options.A: Trade eliminates all labor standards issues
options.B: Competition can push wages down and lead to exploitation in poor working conditions
options.C: Trade always guarantees high wages everywhere
options.D: Labor conditions do not matter in trade

questions.explanation: The presentation includes labor exploitation concerns.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does international trade create monetary gains?

options.A: It helps countries dispose of surplus output, earn foreign exchange, and add to wealth
options.B: It guarantees balanced trade every year
options.C: It eliminates all production costs
options.D: It removes the need for specialization

questions.explanation: The slides identify monetary gains and foreign exchange benefits.

questions.question: How can trade increase employment?

options.A: It eliminates all domestic labor demand
options.B: It only affects customs officers
options.C: It can create jobs in export sectors and through multiplier effects in the wider economy
options.D: It can never create employment

questions.explanation: The slides mention export-led and multiplier-related job creation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What are financial documents?

options.A: Documents proving only origin of goods
options.B: Documents facilitating payment and financing, such as a bill of exchange or letter of credit
options.C: Documents used only to classify goods
options.D: Documents proving only insurance cover

questions.explanation: Financial documents support settlement and finance.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the first main step in an international trade transaction?

options.A: Shipment
options.B: Insurance claim
options.C: Order
options.D: Payment

questions.explanation: The process begins with order.

questions.question: How can persistent trade deficits harm a country?

options.A: They can weaken the currency and force reliance on external debt
options.B: They guarantee stronger currency
options.C: They eliminate all financing needs
options.D: They always increase foreign reserves permanently

questions.explanation: Persistent deficits can create macroeconomic strain.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How does trade break down domestic monopolies?

options.A: It creates monopolies in every sector
options.B: Foreign competition always strengthens domestic monopoly power
options.C: It removes consumer choice
options.D: Foreign competition weakens the ability of one domestic firm to control supply and prices

questions.explanation: Imports can discipline monopolistic domestic firms.

questions.question: A domestic market opens to imports, causing local firms to improve design, innovate, and lower prices to keep customers. Which trade advantage is most directly illustrated?

options.A: Greater competition leading to lower prices, innovation, and quality improvement
options.B: Removal of all employment adjustments
options.C: Elimination of all producer risk
options.D: Guaranteed trade surplus

questions.explanation: The presentation links international trade to competition and better quality.

questions.question: How many member states does the EU have according to the presentation?
questions.explanation: The presentation states 27 EU member states.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is the role of a buying agent?

options.A: To insure the cargo automatically
options.B: To obtain goods abroad on behalf of the importer
options.C: To clear all goods as customs administration
options.D: To issue bills of lading

questions.explanation: A buying agent acts for the importer.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a proforma invoice?

options.A: A final demand for payment after delivery
options.B: A title document proving ownership at sea
options.C: An estimated invoice sent before shipment or delivery, often used as a quotation or for customs purposes
options.D: A bank collection document

questions.explanation: A proforma invoice is preliminary, not final settlement.

questions.question: What is the role of a selling agent?

options.A: To transport the goods by sea
options.B: To classify the goods under HS
options.C: To finance the transaction like a bank
options.D: To sell goods on behalf of the seller

questions.explanation: A selling agent acts for the seller.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a consolidator?

options.A: A party that groups consignments from different consigners for transport and handles documentation and distribution
options.B: A final consumer of the goods
options.C: A customs officer stationed at a port
options.D: A tax authority imposing tariffs

questions.explanation: Consolidators combine shipments.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What determines the number and types of documents used in international trade?

options.A: Only whether sea transport is used
options.B: The sale contract type, exchange controls and regulations, goods characteristics, and means of transport
options.C: Only the buyer’s nationality
options.D: Only whether the seller is a manufacturer

questions.explanation: Multiple commercial and regulatory factors shape the documentation package.

questions.question: How are duties on goods imported into Kenya from the EU phased down under the EPA?

options.A: All tariff lines remain permanently excluded
options.B: All tariff lines become duty free immediately
options.C: Annex II(a) is duty free, Annex II(b) starts at 10% then phases down from Year 7 to 15, Annex II(c) starts at 25% then phases down from Year 12 to 25, and Annex II(d) is excluded
options.D: Only Annex II(d) is duty free from the start

questions.explanation: The presentation gives this phased liberalization structure.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What does accessibility and connectivity mean when choosing a transport mode?

options.A: Only selecting the fastest aircraft
options.B: Considering whether the destination has suitable transport networks and how goods must reach it
options.C: Only comparing insurance rates
options.D: Only choosing the cheapest carrier

questions.explanation: Infrastructure access shapes practical mode choice.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A firm must move high-value pharmaceuticals urgently. Which mode most directly fits the presentation’s guidance?

options.A: Air transport
options.B: Pipeline transport
options.C: Rail transport for bulk minerals
options.D: Sea transport

questions.explanation: Air transport is suited for urgent, high-value, or perishable goods.

questions.question: What is a purchase order?

options.A: An insurance certificate for transit loss
options.B: An internal stock-taking list only
options.C: A written sales contract detailing goods or services, payment terms, delivery date, shipping terms, quantities, and conditions
options.D: A customs manifest for incoming cargo

questions.explanation: The purchase order formalizes the order conditions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: Open account
options.B: Consignment
options.C: A packing list
options.D: A letter of credit

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How can EPAs affect local industries in Kenya?

options.A: Local industries may face stronger competition over time from EU and UK zero-rated products
options.B: Competition from imported goods disappears
options.C: Local industries automatically become monopolies
options.D: All local firms receive guaranteed protection forever

questions.explanation: Competition pressure on local industry is one challenge mentioned.

questions.question: What is the basic duty treatment for goods imported into the UK from Kenya under the EPA?

options.A: The UK retained tariffs on all agricultural products
options.B: Only some tariff lines were liberalized in the UK
options.C: Duties begin at 25% and phase down after 12 years
options.D: Import duties were fully eliminated upon entry into force for all tariff lines

questions.explanation: The presentation states UK duties were fully eliminated from entry into force.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why is the bill of lading important as a document of title?

options.A: Because it replaces customs declarations
options.B: Because it represents ownership of the goods and allows the holder to claim them upon delivery
options.C: Because it always proves insurance cover
options.D: Because it sets exchange rates

questions.explanation: As a title document it can control release of goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a delivery order?

options.A: A bank loan approval note
options.B: A purchase order amendment
options.C: A document issued by the carrier or agent enabling the consignee or forwarding agent to take delivery of cargo
options.D: A customs tariff decision

questions.explanation: Delivery orders facilitate collection from the vessel or port.

questions.question: Which documents may be used at the payment stage?

options.A: Certificate of origin and delivery order only
options.B: Bill of exchange, letter of credit, banker’s draft, and crossed cheque
options.C: Purchase order and contract of sale only
options.D: Manifest, airway bill, and packing list

questions.explanation: These are payment-stage documents.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is the main disadvantage of cash-in-advance for the importer?

options.A: The importer receives title too early
options.B: The importer can inspect the goods before paying
options.C: The importer’s risk is always lower than under an LC
options.D: The seller may fail to deliver after receiving payment

questions.explanation: Advance payment shifts risk to the importer.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Is pricing information required on the packing list?

options.A: Yes, but only for sea freight
options.B: Yes, it must always show full selling price
options.C: No, pricing information is not required
options.D: Yes, but only when goods are insured

questions.explanation: The presentation says pricing does not belong on the packing list.

questions.question: How can over-specialization become a disadvantage?

options.A: Workers and economies become vulnerable if world demand falls or imports undercut domestic production
options.B: It makes demand shocks impossible
options.C: It removes import competition
options.D: It always guarantees permanent stability

questions.explanation: Over-specialization increases vulnerability.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What does FCA mean?

options.A: Seller retains all risks until final buyer delivery
options.B: Free Carrier means the seller delivers the goods, cleared for export, to the carrier nominated by the buyer at the named place
options.C: Seller must unload goods at destination
options.D: Seller delivers only after import clearance

questions.explanation: FCA requires delivery to the buyer’s nominated carrier.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When was the Kenya–EU EPA signed and when did it come into force?

options.A: Signed in December 2020 and in force from January 2021
options.B: Signed in 2024 and in force in 2025
options.C: Signed in 2013 and in force in 2015
options.D: Signed on 18 December 2023 and came into force on 1 July 2024

questions.explanation: These are the dates given in the presentation.

questions.question: How can government policies create international trade advantages?

options.A: Tax and subsidy programs can create production advantages for some products
options.B: By eliminating all transport costs
options.C: By abolishing customs administrations
options.D: By prohibiting imports completely

questions.explanation: The presentation notes that policy differences can create trade advantages.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What share of Kenya’s total imports came from the EU in 2024 according to the presentation?

options.A: 27 percent
options.B: 21.1 percent
options.C: 8.1 percent
options.D: 3.36 percent

questions.explanation: The presentation provides this import share.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which documents may be used at the confirmation stage?

options.A: Invoice, contract of sale, and packing list
options.B: Manifest, rail note, and delivery order
options.C: Insurance certificate only
options.D: Bill of exchange, crossed cheque, and LC

questions.explanation: These appear at confirmation stage.

questions.question: What is one key Incoterms 2020 change regarding FCA?

options.A: FCA became sea-only
options.B: FCA abolished export clearance obligations
options.C: Buyer and seller can agree to issue a bill of lading with an on-board notation, useful for letters of credit
options.D: FCA now requires seller-paid insurance

questions.explanation: This update helps with LC documentary requirements.

tests.title: Customs in International Context - International Trade - Main Test 2

Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the presentation.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: CIF
options.B: CFR
options.C: FOB
options.D: FAS

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What are Incoterms?

options.A: Only sea freight insurance clauses
options.B: A list of customs penalties
options.C: Domestic tax rules for calculating income tax
options.D: Eleven globally recognized three-letter rules published by the ICC defining buyer and seller responsibilities in international trade

questions.explanation: Incoterms allocate responsibilities and risks in trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How can persistent trade deficits harm a country?

options.A: They always increase foreign reserves permanently
options.B: They can weaken the currency and force reliance on external debt
options.C: They guarantee stronger currency
options.D: They eliminate all financing needs

questions.explanation: Persistent deficits can create macroeconomic strain.

questions.question: Why can infant industries suffer from international trade?

options.A: They automatically dominate global markets
options.B: Trade guarantees protection for them
options.C: They may fail to grow because they face strong competition from established foreign firms
options.D: They do not need time to become efficient

questions.explanation: Infant industries may be overwhelmed by mature foreign competitors.

options.A: They guarantee stronger currency

options.B: A road transport document proving receipt, evidencing contract of carriage, invoicing freight, and guiding handling
options.C: A sea freight title document
options.D: An importer’s tax return

questions.explanation: The road consignment note serves several transport functions.

questions.question: Why are sea and rail transport often more cost-effective for bulk cargo?

options.A: Because they are always faster than air
options.B: Because they avoid customs formalities
options.C: Because they require no documentation
options.D: Because they move large volumes over long distances at lower cost

questions.explanation: Bulk cargo usually favors lower-cost modes.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What export strategy mitigation measure is suggested?

options.A: Promote export diversification into manufactured goods and processed agricultural products and support value addition industries
options.B: Avoid all manufacturing growth
options.C: Concentrate exports into fewer raw products only
options.D: Stop participating in foreign markets

questions.explanation: Diversification and value addition are suggested mitigation measures.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the first main step in an international trade transaction?

options.A: Insurance claim
options.B: Payment
options.C: Order
options.D: Shipment

questions.explanation: The process begins with order.

questions.question: What is an export?

options.A: The selling of goods or services produced in one country to another country
options.B: The movement of goods within one customs warehouse
options.C: The internal sale of goods inside one country
options.D: The buying of goods from another country

questions.explanation: An export is an outward sale to another country.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a contract of sale?

options.A: A letter from the bank to the carrier
options.B: A legal contract for exchange of goods, services, or property for money or promise to pay
options.C: A road transport note
options.D: A customs tariff schedule

questions.explanation: The contract of sale governs the sale transaction legally.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does international trade create monetary gains?

options.A: It removes the need for specialization
options.B: It guarantees balanced trade every year
options.C: It eliminates all production costs
options.D: It helps countries dispose of surplus output, earn foreign exchange, and add to wealth

questions.explanation: The slides identify monetary gains and foreign exchange benefits.

questions.question: Which seven Incoterms 2020 rules apply to any mode or modes of transport?

options.A: FAS, FOB, CFR, CIF, DAP, DPU, and DDP
options.B: EXW, FCA, FOB, CFR, CIF, DAP, and DDP
options.C: EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAP, DPU, and DDP
options.D: EXW, CPT, CIP, FAS, FOB, CFR, and CIF

questions.explanation: These seven are the multimodal or any-mode rules.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? When was the Kenya–EU EPA signed and when did it come into force?

options.A: Signed in 2013 and in force in 2015
options.B: Signed on 18 December 2023 and came into force on 1 July 2024
options.C: Signed in 2024 and in force in 2025
options.D: Signed in December 2020 and in force from January 2021

questions.explanation: These are the dates given in the presentation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is an export?

options.A: The selling of goods or services produced in one country to another country
options.B: The internal sale of goods inside one country
options.C: The movement of goods within one customs warehouse
options.D: The buying of goods from another country

questions.explanation: An export is an outward sale to another country.

questions.question: What does flexibility and adaptability mean in transport selection?

options.A: Using one mode forever regardless of conditions
options.B: Avoiding all contingency planning
options.C: The ability to respond to changing demand and disruptions so supply chains stay resilient
options.D: Always choosing road transport only

questions.explanation: Flexibility supports resilience.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How can EPAs affect local industries in Kenya?

options.A: Competition from imported goods disappears
options.B: Local industries automatically become monopolies
options.C: All local firms receive guaranteed protection forever
options.D: Local industries may face stronger competition over time from EU and UK zero-rated products

questions.explanation: Competition pressure on local industry is one challenge mentioned.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why can infant industries suffer from international trade?

options.A: They automatically dominate global markets
options.B: They do not need time to become efficient
options.C: They may fail to grow because they face strong competition from established foreign firms
options.D: Trade guarantees protection for them

questions.explanation: Infant industries may be overwhelmed by mature foreign competitors.

questions.question: What share of Kenya’s total imports came from the EU in 2024 according to the presentation?

options.A: 3.36 percent
options.B: 21.1 percent
options.C: 27 percent
options.D: 8.1 percent

questions.explanation: The presentation provides this import share.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an import?

options.A: The selling of domestic goods abroad
options.B: The buying of goods or services produced in one country from another country
options.C: The transfer of goods between counties
options.D: The classification of goods under HS

questions.explanation: An import is an inward purchase from another country.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which documents may be used at the confirmation stage?

options.A: Invoice, contract of sale, and packing list
options.B: Insurance certificate only
options.C: Bill of exchange, crossed cheque, and LC
options.D: Manifest, rail note, and delivery order

questions.explanation: These appear at confirmation stage.

questions.question: What is an international trade transaction?

options.A: A transaction only within one country
options.B: A domestic warehouse movement only
options.C: A tax dispute between residents
options.D: A transaction involving import-export operations where at least one party is a foreign citizen or foreign juristic person

questions.explanation: The slides define it in relation to foreign parties and trade operations.

questions.question: A domestic market opens to imports, causing local firms to improve design, innovate, and lower prices to keep customers. Which trade advantage is most directly illustrated?

options.A: Elimination of all producer risk
options.B: Removal of all employment adjustments
options.C: Greater competition leading to lower prices, innovation, and quality improvement
options.D: Guaranteed trade surplus

questions.explanation: The presentation links international trade to competition and better quality.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which documents may be used at the confirmation stage?

options.A: Manifest, rail note, and delivery order
options.B: Invoice, contract of sale, and packing list
options.C: Bill of exchange, crossed cheque, and LC
options.D: Insurance certificate only

questions.explanation: These appear at confirmation stage.

questions.question: What is the main risk to the exporter under D/A?

options.A: After the buyer accepts the draft, the exporter remains exposed until actual payment is made
options.B: The exporter bears no financial risk at all
options.C: The exporter receives payment too early
options.D: The exporter always keeps title permanently

questions.explanation: Acceptance is not the same as payment.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What share of Kenya’s total imports came from the EU in 2024 according to the presentation?

options.A: 3.36 percent
options.B: 8.1 percent
options.C: 21.1 percent
options.D: 27 percent

questions.explanation: The presentation provides this import share.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does a positive trade balance mean?

options.A: Imports exceed exports, creating a trade deficit
options.B: The country has abolished tariffs
options.C: Exports exceed imports, creating a trade surplus
options.D: Exports equal imports exactly

questions.explanation: A positive trade balance is a surplus.

questions.question: How does a letter of credit protect the buyer?

options.A: The buyer pays before any documents exist
options.B: The buyer receives guaranteed perfect goods quality
options.C: The buyer can refuse all documents permanently
options.D: No payment obligation arises until shipment or delivery is evidenced as required by the documents

questions.explanation: The buyer benefits from documentary conditions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a tariff?

options.A: A tax on imports or exports
options.B: A freight charge by the carrier
options.C: An insurance premium
options.D: A tax on personal income only

questions.explanation: The presentation defines tariff simply as a tax on imports or exports.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is consignment very risky for the exporter?

options.A: Because the exporter is paid before shipment
options.B: Because payment is not guaranteed and the goods remain under control of an independent foreign distributor or agent
options.C: Because the bank guarantees the sale immediately
options.D: Because the buyer takes no possession of the goods

questions.explanation: Exporter exposure is high under consignment.

questions.question: What are transport documents?

options.A: Seller also must provide all-risk insurance by default
options.B: Buyer does not pay any freight
options.C: Seller keeps risk until cargo is unloaded
options.D: Cost and Freight means the seller pays cost and freight to destination, but risk transfers when goods are placed on board the vessel

questions.explanation: CFR separates freight cost from earlier risk transfer.

questions.question: How can persistent trade deficits harm a country?

options.A: They can weaken the currency and force reliance on external debt
options.B: They always increase foreign reserves permanently
options.C: They guarantee stronger currency
options.D: They eliminate all financing needs

questions.explanation: Persistent deficits can create macroeconomic strain.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a trade bloc?

options.A: A customs office at a border
options.B: A single carrier moving cargo across borders
options.C: A group of countries that establish a free trade area or customs union to promote trade among themselves
options.D: A private bank funding one exporter

questions.explanation: A trade bloc involves multiple countries and preferential trading arrangements.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: Consignment
options.B: A packing list
options.C: Open account
options.D: A letter of credit

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: Is a requisition order the same as a purchase order?

options.A: Yes, once the bank approves it
options.B: No, it is not a purchase order
options.C: Yes, they are always identical
options.D: Yes, but only in sea freight

questions.explanation: The slides clearly distinguish the two.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which documents may be used at the order stage?

options.A: Requisition, purchase order, telex, fax, and proforma invoice
options.B: Bill of lading, airway bill, and manifest
options.C: Certificate of origin and delivery order only
options.D: Insurance policy and cargo receipt only

questions.explanation: These are order-stage documents.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one key Incoterms 2020 change regarding DAT?

options.A: DAT was abolished with no replacement
options.B: DAT was merged into FOB
options.C: DAT was renamed DPU to show delivery unloaded can occur at any place, not only a terminal
options.D: DAT became a payment method

questions.explanation: DAT became DPU in Incoterms 2020.

questions.question: Which four Incoterms 2020 rules apply to sea and inland waterway transport?

options.A: EXW, FCA, CPT, and CIP
options.B: FAS, FOB, CFR, and CIF
options.C: FCA, FOB, CFR, and DAP
options.D: DAP, DPU, DDP, and FOB

questions.explanation: These four are sea/inland waterway rules.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is Documents against Acceptance (D/A)?

options.A: A method where no documents are used
options.B: A method where documents are released only after immediate payment
options.C: A method limited to cash sales only
options.D: A method where the buyer gets documents after accepting a draft or bill of exchange and promising to pay later

questions.explanation: D/A releases documents against accepted future payment.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is bilateral trade?

options.A: Trade between two countries under agreements
options.B: Trade only within one customs territory
options.C: Trade between more than two countries under a global agreement
options.D: Trade involving only air transport

questions.explanation: Bilateral trade is between two countries.

questions.question: When was the Kenya–EU EPA signed and when did it come into force?

options.A: Signed in 2024 and in force in 2025
options.B: Signed in 2013 and in force in 2015
options.C: Signed in December 2020 and in force from January 2021
options.D: Signed on 18 December 2023 and came into force on 1 July 2024

questions.explanation: These are the dates given in the presentation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an HS Code?

options.A: A bank guarantee for payment
options.B: A shipping line reservation code
options.C: A standardized numerical method for classifying traded products
options.D: A method for calculating exchange rates

questions.explanation: HS Codes classify traded products and support tariff determination.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why does D/P provide stronger control in sea freight than in air freight?

options.A: Because customs does not inspect sea cargo
options.B: Because a full set of bills of lading can retain title control, while an airway bill is not a document of title
options.C: Because air freight is always uninsured
options.D: Because sea freight never uses banks

questions.explanation: Title control is stronger where B/Ls are used.

questions.question: What is a trade bloc?

options.A: A group of countries that establish a free trade area or customs union to promote trade among themselves
options.B: A private bank funding one exporter
options.C: A single carrier moving cargo across borders
options.D: A customs office at a border

questions.explanation: A trade bloc involves multiple countries and preferential trading arrangements.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a tariff?

options.A: A freight charge by the carrier
options.B: A tax on personal income only
options.C: An insurance premium
options.D: A tax on imports or exports

questions.explanation: The presentation defines tariff simply as a tax on imports or exports.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a seaway bill?

options.A: A negotiable title document identical to an original bill of lading
options.B: A sea transport document that evidences contract and receipt of goods but does not give title to the goods
options.C: A customs valuation declaration
options.D: A payment guarantee from the bank

questions.explanation: The seaway bill is non-title sea transport documentation.

questions.question: Which seven Incoterms 2020 rules apply to any mode or modes of transport?

options.A: EXW, CPT, CIP, FAS, FOB, CFR, and CIF
options.B: EXW, FCA, FOB, CFR, CIF, DAP, and DDP
options.C: EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAP, DPU, and DDP
options.D: FAS, FOB, CFR, CIF, DAP, DPU, and DDP

questions.explanation: These seven are the multimodal or any-mode rules.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An exporter refuses to ship until money is received. The buyer worries the seller may fail to deliver after getting paid. Which method is this and whose risk is higher before shipment?

options.A: Cash-in-advance, with higher risk for the importer
options.B: Consignment, with higher risk for the bank
options.C: D/P, with no risk for either side
options.D: Open account, with higher risk for the exporter

questions.explanation: Cash-in-advance protects the exporter but exposes the buyer.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a delivery order?

options.A: A customs tariff decision
options.B: A bank loan approval note
options.C: A purchase order amendment
options.D: A document issued by the carrier or agent enabling the consignee or forwarding agent to take delivery of cargo

questions.explanation: Delivery orders facilitate collection from the vessel or port.

questions.question: What is dumping?

options.A: Shipping goods without a bill of lading
options.B: Storing goods temporarily in a foreign port
options.C: Selling goods in a foreign market at a lower price than in the home market, often to drive competitors out
options.D: Selling goods above domestic price to avoid tax

questions.explanation: The presentation defines dumping this way.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why is air transport often chosen?

options.A: Because it is always the cheapest option
options.B: Because it is fast and suitable for urgent, perishable, or high-value goods, though expensive
options.C: Because it automatically transfers title
options.D: Because it is the only option for global trade

questions.explanation: Air transport trades cost for speed.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the five main categories of international trade documents?

options.A: Commercial, transport, financial, insurance, and official/regulatory documents
options.B: Sales, carrier, warehouse, banking, and diplomatic documents
options.C: Export, import, re-export, bilateral, and multilateral documents
options.D: Domestic, tax, customs, labor, and judicial documents

questions.explanation: The presentation groups trade documents into these five categories.

questions.question: Why is a commercial invoice required by customs?

options.A: To issue a bill of lading
options.B: To calculate only exchange rates
options.C: To determine the transaction value of imported goods for duties and taxes
options.D: To authorize export subsidy payment

questions.explanation: Commercial invoices support customs valuation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What does FOB mean?

options.A: Seller delivers only alongside ship
options.B: Free On Board means the seller delivers, cleared for export, when the goods are placed on board the vessel
options.C: Buyer clears goods for export
options.D: Seller must insure the goods under Clause A

questions.explanation: FOB requires on-board delivery.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are insurance documents?

options.A: Documents authorizing customs duty remission
options.B: Documents used to issue HS Codes
options.C: Documents proving trade balance surplus
options.D: Documents protecting against loss or damage during transit, such as an insurance certificate or policy

questions.explanation: Insurance documents cover transit risk.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Kenya reduces tariffs gradually on some EU goods, keeps some lines excluded, but obtains duty-free access for its goods into the EU. Which agreement framework is this?

options.A: Incoterms 2020
options.B: The Kenya–UK EPA alone
options.C: The Kenya–EU EPA
options.D: A documentary collection arrangement

questions.explanation: This matches the Kenya–EU EPA structure described in the presentation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an import?

options.A: The transfer of goods between counties
options.B: The buying of goods or services produced in one country from another country
options.C: The classification of goods under HS
options.D: The selling of domestic goods abroad

questions.explanation: An import is an inward purchase from another country.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Documents against Payment (D/P)?

options.A: A method where documents are released after a promise to pay later
options.B: A method where goods are always sold on consignment
options.C: A method where the exporter pays first
options.D: A method where the buyer’s bank releases documents only when payment is made, usually at sight

questions.explanation: D/P links document release to actual payment.

questions.question: An exporter wants the strongest title control over sea cargo so that documents can be withheld until payment is made. Which transport document best supports this?

options.A: An original bill of lading
options.B: A seaway bill
options.C: An airway bill
options.D: A cargo manifest

questions.explanation: Original bills of lading function as documents of title.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A selling agent and customs administration
options.B: A buying agent and a consolidator
options.C: A consignee and a tax tribunal
options.D: A carrier and a bank

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: A country depends heavily on imported fuel and medicine. A global shock disrupts supply and the economy suffers. Which trade disadvantage is being illustrated?

options.A: Technology transfer
options.B: Breaking down domestic monopolies
options.C: Economies of scale
options.D: Economic dependency and vulnerability

questions.explanation: Reliance on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: Which documents may be used at the order stage?

options.A: Insurance policy and cargo receipt only
options.B: Certificate of origin and delivery order only
options.C: Requisition, purchase order, telex, fax, and proforma invoice
options.D: Bill of lading, airway bill, and manifest

questions.explanation: These are order-stage documents.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What role do banks play in international trade?

options.A: They support trade through payment, capital, loan, and financing services
options.B: They issue packing lists
options.C: They replace the customs administration
options.D: They transport goods by road

questions.explanation: Banks are financial intermediaries in trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is customs clearance in international trade?

options.A: It means only opening a letter of credit
options.B: It means only paying the carrier freight
options.C: It means only calculating trade balance
options.D: It includes not only payment of duties and taxes but also compliance with administrative requirements

questions.explanation: Clearance is broader than tax payment alone.

questions.question: What did the Kenya–UK EPA create?

options.A: A free trade area involving asymmetrical elimination of tariffs on imports originating from either party
options.B: A customs union merging all tariff systems immediately
options.C: A document-only trade arrangement without tariffs
options.D: A common currency area

questions.explanation: The presentation describes the EPA as creating a free trade area.

tests.title: Customs in International Context - International Trade - Main Test 5
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the presentation.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: What is intermodal transportation?

options.A: A customs arrangement for tax-free movement
options.B: Using only one transport mode throughout
options.C: A banking method for trade payment
options.D: The integrated use of different transport modes to improve cargo transfers and supply chain performance

questions.explanation: Intermodal transport combines modes efficiently.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is comparative advantage?

options.A: A situation where tariffs are always zero
options.B: A situation where a nation specializes in goods for which its domestic opportunity cost is lower than that of other nations
options.C: A situation where a nation produces every good absolutely cheaper
options.D: A rule that imports must exceed exports

questions.explanation: Comparative advantage is about lower opportunity cost, not necessarily absolute advantage.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Is an airway bill a document of title?

options.A: No, it is a movement certificate and not a document of title
options.B: Yes, always like an original bill of lading
options.C: It depends only on the Incoterm used
options.D: Yes, but only when endorsed

questions.explanation: The presentation clearly says it is not a title document.

questions.question: When is a telex release relevant?

options.A: Only when no carrier is involved
options.B: Only when goods are moved by pipeline
options.C: Only where an original bill of lading has been issued
options.D: For every transport document including airway bills

questions.explanation: It matters only in relation to original bills of lading.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What insurance change was highlighted for CIP and CIF in Incoterms 2020?

options.A: CIF requires more insurance than CIP by default
options.B: CIP requires maximum all-risk cover under Clause A, while CIF keeps minimum cover under Clause C unless otherwise agreed
options.C: Insurance differences were removed entirely
options.D: Both CIP and CIF require no insurance

questions.explanation: The presentation distinguishes CIP and CIF insurance levels.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why are sea and rail transport often more cost-effective for bulk cargo?

options.A: Because they are always faster than air
options.B: Because they avoid customs formalities
options.C: Because they move large volumes over long distances at lower cost
options.D: Because they require no documentation

questions.explanation: Bulk cargo usually favors lower-cost modes.

questions.question: What does a negative trade balance mean?

options.A: The country has no trade agreements
options.B: Exports exceed imports, creating a trade surplus
options.C: The country has no foreign exchange
options.D: Imports exceed exports, creating a trade deficit

questions.explanation: A negative trade balance is a deficit.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a bill of lading?

options.A: A legal document issued by a carrier to the shipper
options.B: A tax formula for customs valuation
options.C: A bank letter approving a loan
options.D: An internal requisition note

questions.explanation: The bill of lading is a core sea transport document.

questions.question: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A consignee and a tax tribunal
options.B: A selling agent and customs administration
options.C: A carrier and a bank
options.D: A buying agent and a consolidator

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: CIF
options.B: FAS
options.C: CFR
options.D: FOB

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What determines the number and types of documents used in international trade?

options.A: Only the buyer’s nationality
options.B: The sale contract type, exchange controls and regulations, goods characteristics, and means of transport
options.C: Only whether the seller is a manufacturer
options.D: Only whether sea transport is used

questions.explanation: Multiple commercial and regulatory factors shape the documentation package.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: Open account
options.B: Consignment
options.C: A letter of credit
options.D: A packing list

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: What is confirmation in international trade?

options.A: The customs release of cargo
options.B: A document from the seller to the buyer confirming the terms of delivery
options.C: The bank's refusal to finance the transaction
options.D: The consignee's transport booking

questions.explanation: Confirmation records the seller's acceptance or confirmation of terms.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is international trade?

options.A: The exchange of goods, services, and capital across national borders
options.B: The exchange of goods only within one country
options.C: The movement of people without goods or services
options.D: The taxation of domestic production only

questions.explanation: The presentation defines international trade as exchange across national borders involving goods, services, and capital.

questions.question: A buyer sends a request for goods, the seller replies confirming delivery terms, then the goods are transported, and only after that settlement occurs. What is the correct sequence?

options.A: Order, shipment, confirmation, payment
options.B: Confirmation, order, payment, shipment
options.C: Shipment, order, confirmation, payment
options.D: Order, confirmation, shipment, payment

questions.explanation: The presentation gives this main sequence.

questions.question: How does international trade increase competition?

options.A: It guarantees monopoly power for domestic firms
options.B: It reduces choice for consumers in all cases
options.C: It lowers prices, improves responsiveness to consumer needs, and expands variety
options.D: It always raises prices

questions.explanation: The presentation emphasizes competition benefits.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a delivery order?

options.A: A customs tariff decision
options.B: A document issued by the carrier or agent enabling the consignee or forwarding agent to take delivery of cargo
options.C: A bank loan approval note
options.D: A purchase order amendment

questions.explanation: Delivery orders facilitate collection from the vessel or port.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied
options.B: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.C: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.D: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: Customs needs a document to assess duties and taxes based on transaction value, while the warehouse team needs a detailed list of package contents without prices. Which two documents fit these needs?

options.A: Commercial invoice and packing list
options.B: Road consignment note and consular invoice
options.C: Proforma invoice and telex release
options.D: Manifest and crossed cheque

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is bilateral trade?

options.A: Trade involving only air transport
options.B: Trade only within one customs territory
options.C: Trade between two countries under agreements
options.D: Trade between more than two countries under a global agreement

questions.explanation: Bilateral trade is between two countries.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a tariff?

options.A: A tax on imports or exports
options.B: A tax on personal income only
options.C: An insurance premium
options.D: A freight charge by the carrier

questions.explanation: The presentation defines tariff simply as a tax on imports or exports.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A domestic market opens to imports, causing local firms to improve design, innovate, and lower prices to keep customers. Which trade advantage is most directly illustrated?

options.A: Guaranteed trade surplus
options.B: Elimination of all producer risk
options.C: Greater competition leading to lower prices, innovation, and quality improvement
options.D: Removal of all employment adjustments

questions.explanation: The presentation links international trade to competition and better quality.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How can persistent trade deficits harm a country?

options.A: They guarantee stronger currency
options.B: They can weaken the currency and force reliance on external debt
options.C: They always increase foreign reserves permanently
options.D: They eliminate all financing needs

questions.explanation: Persistent deficits can create macroeconomic strain.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How can international trade contribute to environmental degradation?

options.A: Only domestic trade can cause pollution
options.B: Environmental rules are irrelevant to trade
options.C: Trade automatically improves all environmental outcomes
options.D: Production may relocate to countries with weak environmental rules, causing pollution and unsustainable resource use

questions.explanation: The slides identify pollution and weak regulation risks.

questions.question: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.B: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.C: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.D: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What does CIP mean?

options.A: Buyer must insure the goods from origin
options.B: Seller must only pay inland taxes
options.C: Seller unloads the goods at destination
options.D: Carriage and Insurance Paid To means the seller has CPT obligations and must also procure insurance for the buyer’s risk during carriage

questions.explanation: CIP adds seller-procured insurance.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is trade balance?

options.A: The difference between a country's exports and imports
options.B: The sum of all tariffs charged in a year
options.C: The value of goods under customs control
options.D: The ratio of duties to VAT

questions.explanation: Trade balance compares exports and imports.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to deliver goods to the destination, unload them there, and place them at the buyer’s disposal. Which Incoterm best fits?

options.A: DAP
options.B: DPU
options.C: EXW
options.D: FOB

questions.explanation: DPU requires the seller to unload at destination.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a trade bloc?

options.A: A group of countries that establish a free trade area or customs union to promote trade among themselves
options.B: A single carrier moving cargo across borders
options.C: A private bank funding one exporter
options.D: A customs office at a border

questions.explanation: A trade bloc involves multiple countries and preferential trading arrangements.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a trade bloc?

options.A: A single carrier moving cargo across borders
options.B: A private bank funding one exporter
options.C: A group of countries that establish a free trade area or customs union to promote trade among themselves
options.D: A customs office at a border

questions.explanation: A trade bloc involves multiple countries and preferential trading arrangements.

questions.question: What labor and ethical concerns can arise from international trade?

options.A: Trade eliminates all labor standards issues
options.B: Competition can push wages down and lead to exploitation in poor working conditions
options.C: Labor conditions do not matter in trade
options.D: Trade always guarantees high wages everywhere

questions.explanation: The presentation includes labor exploitation concerns.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What are the main transport modes listed in the presentation?

options.A: Only air and sea
options.B: Only air, sea, and pipeline
options.C: Only rail and road
options.D: Air, maritime, rail, road, pipeline, and intermodal transportation

questions.explanation: The presentation lists six major transport modes.

questions.question: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: FAS
options.B: FOB
options.C: CIF
options.D: CFR

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: What are insurance documents?

options.A: Documents protecting against loss or damage during transit, such as an insurance certificate or policy
options.B: Documents used to issue HS Codes
options.C: Documents authorizing customs duty remission
options.D: Documents proving trade balance surplus

questions.explanation: Insurance documents cover transit risk.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is one challenge of the Kenya–EU and Kenya–UK EPAs?

options.A: They automatically eliminate all domestic tax concerns
options.B: Balancing EPA implementation with the EAC Common External Tariff can be difficult
options.C: They remove all competition pressures on local firms
options.D: There are no implementation challenges at all

questions.explanation: The presentation identifies CET compatibility as a challenge.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why do countries engage in international trade because of unequal distribution of resources?

options.A: Because customs administrations require it
options.B: Because trade removes all differences in climate and geography
options.C: Because every country can produce everything efficiently
options.D: Because countries do not all have the same resources or capacity to satisfy their own needs and wants

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is a basic reason for trade.

questions.question: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A buying agent and a consolidator
options.B: A carrier and a bank

options.D: A consignee and a tax tribunal

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is trade balance?

options.B: The value of goods under customs control

questions.explanation: Trade balance compares exports and imports.

questions.question: A seller agrees to deliver goods to the destination, unload them there, and place them at the buyer’s disposal. Which Incoterm best fits?

options.A: EXW

questions.question: What does EXW mean?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an HS Code?

options.C: A method for calculating exchange rates
options.D: A standardized numerical method for classifying traded products

questions.explanation: HS Codes classify traded products and support tariff determination.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A firm must move high-value pharmaceuticals urgently. Which mode most directly fits the presentation’s guidance?

options.A: Pipeline transport
options.B: Rail transport for bulk minerals

options.D: Sea transport

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Who is the seller in an international trade transaction?

questions.question: Customs needs a document to assess duties and taxes based on transaction value, while the warehouse team needs a detailed list of package contents without prices. Which two documents fit these needs?

options.A: Road consignment note and consular invoice

options.D: Proforma invoice and telex release

questions.explanation: Commercial invoice supports valuation; packing list supports physical cargo handling.

questions.question: What is consignment in international trade?

questions.explanation: Consignment postpones exporter payment until downstream sale.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How does international trade increase competition?

options.A: It reduces choice for consumers in all cases
options.B: It lowers prices, improves responsiveness to consumer needs, and expands variety
options.C: It always raises prices
options.D: It guarantees monopoly power for domestic firms

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which seven Incoterms 2020 rules apply to any mode or modes of transport?

questions.question: How does specialization create economies of scale?

options.A: Air transport
options.B: Sea transport
options.C: Pipeline transport
options.D: Rail transport for bulk minerals

questions.explanation: Air transport is suited for urgent, high-value, or perishable goods.

questions.question: What is Documents against Payment (D/P)?

options.B: A method where the exporter pays first

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is one key Incoterms 2020 change regarding DAT?

options.A: DAT was merged into FOB
options.B: DAT became a payment method
options.C: DAT was renamed DPU to show delivery unloaded can occur at any place, not only a terminal
options.D: DAT was abolished with no replacement

questions.explanation: DAT became DPU in Incoterms 2020.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How can persistent trade deficits harm a country?

options.A: They can weaken the currency and force reliance on external debt
options.B: They always increase foreign reserves permanently
options.C: They guarantee stronger currency
options.D: They eliminate all financing needs

questions.question: How is a proforma invoice different from a commercial invoice?

options.C: A commercial invoice is not used by customs
options.D: There is no difference between them

options.B: FOB
options.C: CFR

options.A: A packing list

options.D: A letter of credit

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: What is a purchase order?

options.B: An internal stock-taking list only

options.D: An insurance certificate for transit loss

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How does competition from trade improve quality?

options.A: It lowers quality by definition
options.B: It encourages innovation, better design, and new technologies
options.C: It removes any need to innovate
options.D: It guarantees identical products everywhere

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does trade encourage specialization?

options.A: It eliminates differences in productivity
options.B: It requires identical production structures in all countries
options.C: It forces every country to produce everything
options.D: It encourages countries to produce goods and services they can make more efficiently and at lower opportunity cost

questions.explanation: Trade and specialization are closely linked.

questions.question: Who is the seller in an international trade transaction?

options.A: The party that may also be the supplier, manufacturer, and/or exporter of the goods
options.B: Only the carrier
options.C: Only the customs administration
options.D: Only the bank

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an HS Code?

options.A: A method for calculating exchange rates
options.B: A standardized numerical method for classifying traded products
options.C: A shipping line reservation code
options.D: A bank guarantee for payment

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How is a proforma invoice different from a commercial invoice?

options.C: Only customs officers at the border
options.D: Only insurers protecting cargo

questions.explanation: Carriers move the goods.

options.A: FOB
options.B: DAP
options.C: DPU
options.D: EXW

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why were Incoterms developed?

options.A: To classify goods under HS

options.C: To replace all contracts of sale
options.D: To determine trade balance

questions.question: What is an airway bill?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is bilateral trade?

options.C: Trade between more than two countries under a global agreement
options.D: Trade only within one customs territory

options.B: It only affects transport, not production
options.C: It prevents knowledge from crossing borders
options.D: It spreads innovations, knowledge, methods, machinery, software, and organizational practices

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is a basic reason for trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one key limitation of banks under a letter of credit?

options.A: Banks deal with documents, not the actual quality of the goods
options.B: Banks replace carriers in all trade flows
options.C: Banks physically inspect and guarantee every product
options.D: Banks determine Incoterms by law

questions.explanation: Banks examine documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.B: A carrier and a bank

options.D: A buying agent and a consolidator

options.B: Only the transport leg of trade
options.C: The network of businesses and organizations involved in creation and delivery of a product or service from suppliers to end-users
options.D: A single warehouse storing goods

questions.explanation: A supply chain spans the full network from inputs to end-users.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does FAS mean?

options.A: Seller clears goods for import

options.C: Seller places goods on board the vessel
options.D: Seller delivers to first inland carrier only

questions.explanation: FAS requires delivery alongside ship.

questions.question: What is a requisition order?

options.B: A customs declaration of value
options.C: An internal document notifying the purchasing department of items needed, quantity, and required time frame

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is entrepôt or re-export trade?

options.D: Only the insurer

options.C: A bank draft for trade settlement
options.D: A sea title document

questions.explanation: The airway bill is an air carriage document.
