questions.question: What is a supply chain?

options.A: Only customs clearance procedures
options.B: The network of businesses and organizations involved in creation and delivery of a product or service from suppliers to end-users
options.C: Only the transport leg of trade
options.D: A single warehouse storing goods

questions.explanation: A supply chain spans the full network from inputs to end-users.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What are insurance documents?

options.A: Documents protecting against loss or damage during transit, such as an insurance certificate or policy
options.B: Documents used to issue HS Codes
options.C: Documents proving trade balance surplus
options.D: Documents authorizing customs duty remission

questions.explanation: Insurance documents cover transit risk.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is bilateral trade?

options.A: Trade only within one customs territory
options.B: Trade involving only air transport
options.C: Trade between more than two countries under a global agreement
options.D: Trade between two countries under agreements

questions.explanation: Bilateral trade is between two countries.

questions.question: What is shipment in international trade?

options.A: Only inland warehouse storage
options.B: Transportation of goods by marine, air, rail, road, or post
options.C: Calculation of VAT at importation
options.D: Negotiation of subsidy policy

questions.explanation: Shipment is the movement stage.

questions.question: An exporter refuses to ship until money is received. The buyer worries the seller may fail to deliver after getting paid. Which method is this and whose risk is higher before shipment?

options.A: Open account, with higher risk for the exporter
options.B: D/P, with no risk for either side
options.C: Consignment, with higher risk for the bank
options.D: Cash-in-advance, with higher risk for the importer

questions.explanation: Cash-in-advance protects the exporter but exposes the buyer.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: CFR
options.B: FAS
options.C: CIF
options.D: FOB

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: Why is the bill of lading important as a document of title?

options.A: Because it represents ownership of the goods and allows the holder to claim them upon delivery
options.B: Because it sets exchange rates
options.C: Because it replaces customs declarations
options.D: Because it always proves insurance cover

questions.explanation: As a title document it can control release of goods.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A firm must move high-value pharmaceuticals urgently. Which mode most directly fits the presentation’s guidance?

options.A: Rail transport for bulk minerals
options.B: Sea transport
options.C: Air transport
options.D: Pipeline transport

questions.explanation: Air transport is suited for urgent, high-value, or perishable goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a consular invoice?

options.A: A customs bond for warehouse storage
options.B: A rail transport document
options.C: A domestic sales receipt
options.D: A commercial invoice sighted, signed, and stamped by the consul of the importing country resident in the exporting country

questions.explanation: The consular invoice is a commercial invoice with consular authentication.

questions.question: What is a delivery order?

options.A: A document issued by the carrier or agent enabling the consignee or forwarding agent to take delivery of cargo
options.B: A bank loan approval note
options.C: A purchase order amendment
options.D: A customs tariff decision

questions.explanation: Delivery orders facilitate collection from the vessel or port.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is payment in international trade?

options.A: The transport stage by sea or air
options.B: The settlement stage, depending on the parties' agreement
options.C: The manufacturing stage before shipment
options.D: The customs classification stage only

questions.explanation: Payment settles the transaction.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country depends heavily on imported fuel and medicine. A global shock disrupts supply and the economy suffers. Which trade disadvantage is being illustrated?

options.A: Economies of scale
options.B: Technology transfer
options.C: Economic dependency and vulnerability
options.D: Breaking down domestic monopolies

questions.explanation: Reliance on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: What does EXW mean?

options.A: Seller must clear goods for import and pay duties
options.B: Seller delivers goods on board a vessel
options.C: Seller must unload goods at destination
options.D: Ex Works means the seller places goods at the buyer’s disposal at a named place without loading them and generally without export clearance as part of delivery

questions.explanation: EXW places minimal responsibility on the seller.

tests.title: Customs in International Context - International Trade - Main Test 11
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the presentation.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: What is the formula for calculating trade balance?

options.A: Total Value of Imports minus Total Value of Exports
options.B: Tariffs minus transport costs
options.C: Exports plus imports
options.D: Total Value of Exports minus Total Value of Imports

questions.explanation: Trade balance is exports less imports.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.B: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.C: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied
options.D: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is economic dependency and vulnerability in international trade?

options.A: Dependence only on domestic agriculture
options.B: Dependence only on inland transport
options.C: Heavy reliance on imports for critical goods such as food, energy, medicine, or strategic materials
options.D: Dependence only on customs revenues

questions.explanation: Dependency on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A consignee and a tax tribunal
options.B: A buying agent and a consolidator
options.C: A carrier and a bank
options.D: A selling agent and customs administration

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an export?

options.A: The movement of goods within one customs warehouse
options.B: The internal sale of goods inside one country
options.C: The buying of goods from another country
options.D: The selling of goods or services produced in one country to another country

questions.explanation: An export is an outward sale to another country.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An exporter wants the strongest title control over sea cargo so that documents can be withheld until payment is made. Which transport document best supports this?

options.A: A cargo manifest
options.B: An original bill of lading
options.C: An airway bill
options.D: A seaway bill

questions.explanation: Original bills of lading function as documents of title.

questions.question: A buyer sends a request for goods, the seller replies confirming delivery terms, then the goods are transported, and only after that settlement occurs. What is the correct sequence?

options.A: Order, shipment, confirmation, payment
options.B: Shipment, order, confirmation, payment
options.C: Order, confirmation, shipment, payment
options.D: Confirmation, order, payment, shipment

questions.explanation: The presentation gives this main sequence.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is comparative advantage?

options.A: A situation where a nation specializes in goods for which its domestic opportunity cost is lower than that of other nations
options.B: A situation where tariffs are always zero
options.C: A situation where a nation produces every good absolutely cheaper
options.D: A rule that imports must exceed exports

questions.explanation: Comparative advantage is about lower opportunity cost, not necessarily absolute advantage.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is economic dependency and vulnerability in international trade?

options.A: Dependence only on inland transport
options.B: Dependence only on domestic agriculture
options.C: Heavy reliance on imports for critical goods such as food, energy, medicine, or strategic materials
options.D: Dependence only on customs revenues

questions.explanation: Dependency on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Kenya reduces tariffs gradually on some EU goods, keeps some lines excluded, but obtains duty-free access for its goods into the EU. Which agreement framework is this?

options.A: The Kenya–EU EPA
options.B: The Kenya–UK EPA alone
options.C: A documentary collection arrangement
options.D: Incoterms 2020

questions.explanation: This matches the Kenya–EU EPA structure described in the presentation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a tariff?

options.A: A freight charge by the carrier
options.B: A tax on imports or exports
options.C: A tax on personal income only
options.D: An insurance premium

questions.explanation: The presentation defines tariff simply as a tax on imports or exports.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is maritime transportation important to globalization?

options.A: Because it replaces all other modes
options.B: Because it eliminates the need for inland transport
options.C: Because it is relevant only to local trade
options.D: Because it enables movement of goods between continents and supports the global supply chain

questions.explanation: Sea transport underpins global cargo movement.

questions.question: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.B: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied
options.C: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.D: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: CFR
options.B: FOB
options.C: CIF
options.D: FAS

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: A seller agrees to deliver goods to the destination, unload them there, and place them at the buyer’s disposal. Which Incoterm best fits?

options.A: FOB
options.B: DAP
options.C: EXW
options.D: DPU

questions.explanation: DPU requires the seller to unload at destination.

questions.question: Why is an open account risky for the exporter?

options.A: Because customs takes ownership on arrival
options.B: Because goods are delivered before payment is due, creating non-payment risk
options.C: Because the bank holds title to the goods
options.D: Because the buyer must pay too early

questions.explanation: Exporter risk is high under open account terms.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Which documents may be used at the confirmation stage?

options.A: Insurance certificate only
options.B: Bill of exchange, crossed cheque, and LC
options.C: Manifest, rail note, and delivery order
options.D: Invoice, contract of sale, and packing list

questions.explanation: These appear at confirmation stage.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.B: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.C: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.D: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: What is the role of a buying agent?

options.A: To obtain goods abroad on behalf of the importer
options.B: To issue bills of lading
options.C: To insure the cargo automatically
options.D: To clear all goods as customs administration

questions.explanation: A buying agent acts for the importer.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why does D/P provide stronger control in sea freight than in air freight?

options.A: Because air freight is always uninsured
options.B: Because customs does not inspect sea cargo
options.C: Because a full set of bills of lading can retain title control, while an airway bill is not a document of title
options.D: Because sea freight never uses banks

questions.explanation: Title control is stronger where B/Ls are used.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Who are carriers in international trade?

options.A: Persons who undertake or procure carriage by rail, road, sea, air, inland waterway, or combinations
options.B: Only customs officers at the border
options.C: Only banks that pay exporters
options.D: Only insurers protecting cargo

questions.explanation: Carriers move the goods.

questions.question: What is economic dependency and vulnerability in international trade?

options.A: Dependence only on inland transport
options.B: Heavy reliance on imports for critical goods such as food, energy, medicine, or strategic materials
options.C: Dependence only on customs revenues
options.D: Dependence only on domestic agriculture

questions.explanation: Dependency on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: A packing list
options.B: Consignment
options.C: A letter of credit
options.D: Open account

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When was the Kenya–EU EPA signed and when did it come into force?

options.A: Signed in 2024 and in force in 2025
options.B: Signed in December 2020 and in force from January 2021
options.C: Signed in 2013 and in force in 2015
options.D: Signed on 18 December 2023 and came into force on 1 July 2024

questions.explanation: These are the dates given in the presentation.

questions.question: Why is an open account risky for the exporter?

options.A: Because the bank holds title to the goods
options.B: Because goods are delivered before payment is due, creating non-payment risk
options.C: Because the buyer must pay too early
options.D: Because customs takes ownership on arrival

questions.explanation: Exporter risk is high under open account terms.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An exporter wants the strongest title control over sea cargo so that documents can be withheld until payment is made. Which transport document best supports this?

options.A: A seaway bill
options.B: An airway bill
options.C: A cargo manifest
options.D: An original bill of lading

questions.explanation: Original bills of lading function as documents of title.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How much total trade took place between Kenya and the EU in 2024 according to the presentation?

options.A: €3.36 billion
options.B: €21.1 billion
options.C: €8.1 billion
options.D: €1.0 billion

questions.explanation: This total trade value is listed in the slides.

questions.question: What does CPT mean?

options.A: Buyer pays freight but seller keeps title
options.B: Seller keeps risk until final unloading
options.C: Carriage Paid To means the seller pays carriage to destination but risk transfers after delivery to the carrier
options.D: Seller also has no responsibility for export clearance

questions.explanation: CPT splits freight cost from risk transfer.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? How does a letter of credit protect the buyer?

options.A: The buyer pays before any documents exist
options.B: No payment obligation arises until shipment or delivery is evidenced as required by the documents
options.C: The buyer receives guaranteed perfect goods quality
options.D: The buyer can refuse all documents permanently

questions.explanation: The buyer benefits from documentary conditions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A policymaker worries that lower tariffs under trade agreements could reduce customs revenue and expose local manufacturers to stronger foreign competition. Which pair of concerns from the presentation does this reflect?

options.A: Improved title control and faster customs clearance
options.B: Increased trade balance certainty and reduced technology transfer
options.C: Lower environmental standards and stronger monopoly protection
options.D: Revenue loss and competition pressure on local industries

questions.explanation: These are two key EPA-related challenges listed in the slides.

questions.question: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A buying agent and a consolidator
options.B: A carrier and a bank
options.C: A selling agent and customs administration
options.D: A consignee and a tax tribunal

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Kenya reduces tariffs gradually on some EU goods, keeps some lines excluded, but obtains duty-free access for its goods into the EU. Which agreement framework is this?

options.A: The Kenya–UK EPA alone
options.B: A documentary collection arrangement
options.C: The Kenya–EU EPA
options.D: Incoterms 2020

questions.explanation: This matches the Kenya–EU EPA structure described in the presentation.

questions.question: Customs needs a document to assess duties and taxes based on transaction value, while the warehouse team needs a detailed list of package contents without prices. Which two documents fit these needs?

options.A: Road consignment note and consular invoice
options.B: Proforma invoice and telex release
options.C: Manifest and crossed cheque
options.D: Commercial invoice and packing list

questions.explanation: Commercial invoice supports valuation; packing list supports physical cargo handling.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A firm must move high-value pharmaceuticals urgently. Which mode most directly fits the presentation’s guidance?

options.A: Sea transport
options.B: Rail transport for bulk minerals
options.C: Air transport
options.D: Pipeline transport

questions.explanation: Air transport is suited for urgent, high-value, or perishable goods.

questions.question: A country depends heavily on imported fuel and medicine. A global shock disrupts supply and the economy suffers. Which trade disadvantage is being illustrated?

options.A: Economies of scale
options.B: Economic dependency and vulnerability
options.C: Technology transfer
options.D: Breaking down domestic monopolies

questions.explanation: Reliance on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does DAP mean?

options.A: Delivered at Place means the seller delivers at the agreed destination, not cleared for import and not unloaded
options.B: Buyer bears all costs from origin
options.C: Seller delivers only to the first carrier
options.D: Seller unloads the goods and clears them for import

questions.explanation: DAP leaves import clearance and unloading outside seller delivery.

questions.question: A domestic market opens to imports, causing local firms to improve design, innovate, and lower prices to keep customers. Which trade advantage is most directly illustrated?

options.A: Guaranteed trade surplus
options.B: Removal of all employment adjustments
options.C: Greater competition leading to lower prices, innovation, and quality improvement
options.D: Elimination of all producer risk

questions.explanation: The presentation links international trade to competition and better quality.

options.A: Lower environmental standards and stronger monopoly protection
options.B: Improved title control and faster customs clearance
options.C: Increased trade balance certainty and reduced technology transfer
options.D: Revenue loss and competition pressure on local industries

questions.explanation: These are two key EPA-related challenges listed in the slides.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does a letter of credit protect the buyer?

options.A: The buyer pays before any documents exist
options.B: No payment obligation arises until shipment or delivery is evidenced as required by the documents
options.C: The buyer can refuse all documents permanently
options.D: The buyer receives guaranteed perfect goods quality

questions.explanation: The buyer benefits from documentary conditions.

options.A: CIF
options.B: FOB
options.C: CFR
options.D: FAS

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: A domestic market opens to imports, causing local firms to improve design, innovate, and lower prices to keep customers. Which trade advantage is most directly illustrated?

options.A: Guaranteed trade surplus
options.B: Removal of all employment adjustments
options.C: Greater competition leading to lower prices, innovation, and quality improvement
options.D: Elimination of all producer risk

questions.explanation: The presentation links international trade to competition and better quality.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A carrier and a bank
options.B: A consignee and a tax tribunal
options.C: A selling agent and customs administration
options.D: A buying agent and a consolidator

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: What is an Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA)?

options.A: A trade agreement designed to promote economic cooperation, reduce trade barriers, and improve market access
options.B: A transport mode for cross-border cargo
options.C: A financing tool used only by banks
options.D: A domestic tax law replacing customs duties

questions.explanation: The presentation defines EPA in these terms.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is Documents against Payment (D/P)?

options.A: A method where documents are released after a promise to pay later
options.B: A method where the buyer’s bank releases documents only when payment is made, usually at sight
options.C: A method where goods are always sold on consignment
options.D: A method where the exporter pays first

questions.explanation: D/P links document release to actual payment.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is an airway bill?

options.A: A bank draft for trade settlement
options.B: A customs guarantee
options.C: A non-negotiable transport document showing the contract between shipper and carrier for airport-to-airport carriage
options.D: A sea title document

questions.explanation: The airway bill is an air carriage document.

questions.question: What is a proforma invoice?

options.A: A final demand for payment after delivery
options.B: An estimated invoice sent before shipment or delivery, often used as a quotation or for customs purposes
options.C: A bank collection document
options.D: A title document proving ownership at sea

questions.explanation: A proforma invoice is preliminary, not final settlement.

questions.question: An exporter refuses to ship until money is received. The buyer worries the seller may fail to deliver after getting paid. Which method is this and whose risk is higher before shipment?

options.A: Open account, with higher risk for the exporter
options.B: Consignment, with higher risk for the bank
options.C: D/P, with no risk for either side
options.D: Cash-in-advance, with higher risk for the importer

questions.explanation: Cash-in-advance protects the exporter but exposes the buyer.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a supply chain?

options.A: The network of businesses and organizations involved in creation and delivery of a product or service from suppliers to end-users
options.B: A single warehouse storing goods
options.C: Only customs clearance procedures
options.D: Only the transport leg of trade

questions.explanation: A supply chain spans the full network from inputs to end-users.

questions.question: How does specialization create economies of scale?

options.A: Producing fewer goods always increases average costs
options.B: Producing at small scale lowers all costs automatically
options.C: Producing a narrower range of goods in larger volumes lowers average costs
options.D: Economies of scale arise only from tariffs

questions.explanation: Higher volume in specialized production can reduce average costs.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? When is a telex release relevant?

options.A: Only when no carrier is involved
options.B: For every transport document including airway bills
options.C: Only when goods are moved by pipeline
options.D: Only where an original bill of lading has been issued

questions.explanation: It matters only in relation to original bills of lading.

questions.question: A seller pays freight to the destination port and also procures at least minimum cargo insurance for sea transport. Which sea rule fits best?

options.A: CFR
options.B: FOB
options.C: FAS
options.D: CIF

questions.explanation: CIF adds insurance to CFR-type obligations.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: Open account
options.B: A packing list
options.C: A letter of credit
options.D: Consignment

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the main disadvantage of cash-in-advance for the importer?

options.A: The seller may fail to deliver after receiving payment
options.B: The importer receives title too early
options.C: The importer’s risk is always lower than under an LC
options.D: The importer can inspect the goods before paying

questions.explanation: Advance payment shifts risk to the importer.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs needs a document to assess duties and taxes based on transaction value, while the warehouse team needs a detailed list of package contents without prices. Which two documents fit these needs?

options.A: Manifest and crossed cheque
options.B: Proforma invoice and telex release
options.C: Road consignment note and consular invoice
options.D: Commercial invoice and packing list

questions.explanation: Commercial invoice supports valuation; packing list supports physical cargo handling.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is one key limitation of banks under a letter of credit?

options.A: Banks deal with documents, not the actual quality of the goods
options.B: Banks replace carriers in all trade flows
options.C: Banks physically inspect and guarantee every product
options.D: Banks determine Incoterms by law

questions.explanation: Banks examine documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which documents may be used at the shipment stage?

options.A: Only insurance policy and proforma invoice
options.B: Bill of lading, rail consignment note, airway bill, road consignment note, and customs cargo control document
options.C: Only cheque and banker’s draft
options.D: Only purchase order and requisition

questions.explanation: These are shipment-related documents.

questions.question: What is Documents against Acceptance (D/A)?

options.A: A method where documents are released only after immediate payment
options.B: A method where no documents are used
options.C: A method where the buyer gets documents after accepting a draft or bill of exchange and promising to pay later
options.D: A method limited to cash sales only

questions.explanation: D/A releases documents against accepted future payment.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an international trade transaction?

options.A: A tax dispute between residents
options.B: A transaction involving import-export operations where at least one party is a foreign citizen or foreign juristic person
options.C: A domestic warehouse movement only
options.D: A transaction only within one country

questions.explanation: The slides define it in relation to foreign parties and trade operations.

questions.question: A policymaker worries that lower tariffs under trade agreements could reduce customs revenue and expose local manufacturers to stronger foreign competition. Which pair of concerns from the presentation does this reflect?

options.A: Revenue loss and competition pressure on local industries
options.B: Lower environmental standards and stronger monopoly protection
options.C: Increased trade balance certainty and reduced technology transfer
options.D: Improved title control and faster customs clearance

questions.explanation: These are two key EPA-related challenges listed in the slides.

questions.question: What is one key Incoterms 2020 change regarding FCA?

options.A: FCA became sea-only
options.B: FCA abolished export clearance obligations
options.C: FCA now requires seller-paid insurance
options.D: Buyer and seller can agree to issue a bill of lading with an on-board notation, useful for letters of credit

questions.explanation: This update helps with LC documentary requirements.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why is an open account risky for the exporter?

options.A: Because the bank holds title to the goods
options.B: Because customs takes ownership on arrival
options.C: Because goods are delivered before payment is due, creating non-payment risk
options.D: Because the buyer must pay too early

questions.explanation: Exporter risk is high under open account terms.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does competition from trade improve quality?

options.A: It guarantees identical products everywhere
options.B: It removes any need to innovate
options.C: It lowers quality by definition
options.D: It encourages innovation, better design, and new technologies

questions.explanation: Competition can raise quality through innovation.

questions.question: What is dumping?

options.A: Selling goods in a foreign market at a lower price than in the home market, often to drive competitors out
options.B: Selling goods above domestic price to avoid tax
options.C: Storing goods temporarily in a foreign port
options.D: Shipping goods without a bill of lading

questions.explanation: The presentation defines dumping this way.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What does an overseas shipping or airline agent do?

options.A: They issue certificates of origin only
options.B: They arrange transportation, make bookings, and oversee loading and passage of goods
options.C: They determine corporate tax rates
options.D: They replace the carrier

questions.explanation: The agent helps organize overseas carriage.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a house airway bill?

options.A: A final payment order by the bank
options.B: A customs certificate proving origin
options.C: A document issued by a freight forwarder or consolidator to a shipper whose goods move with other cargo as one consignment
options.D: A road freight title document

questions.explanation: House airway bills arise in consolidated shipments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer hires one firm to source goods abroad and another party to combine multiple shipments from different exporters into one transport lot. Which pair of players best fits this scenario?

options.A: A selling agent and customs administration
options.B: A buying agent and a consolidator
options.C: A carrier and a bank
options.D: A consignee and a tax tribunal

questions.explanation: Buying agents source goods; consolidators group consignments.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is an original bill of lading?

options.A: The primary sea transport document serving as evidence of contract, receipt of goods, and legal title to the goods
options.B: An air cargo movement certificate
options.C: A road note used for truck freight
options.D: A preliminary sales quotation

questions.explanation: The original bill of lading carries title significance.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A firm must move high-value pharmaceuticals urgently. Which mode most directly fits the presentation’s guidance?

options.A: Sea transport
options.B: Rail transport for bulk minerals
options.C: Pipeline transport
options.D: Air transport

questions.explanation: Air transport is suited for urgent, high-value, or perishable goods.

questions.question: Customs needs a document to assess duties and taxes based on transaction value, while the warehouse team needs a detailed list of package contents without prices. Which two documents fit these needs?

options.A: Proforma invoice and telex release
options.B: Road consignment note and consular invoice
options.C: Commercial invoice and packing list
options.D: Manifest and crossed cheque

questions.explanation: Commercial invoice supports valuation; packing list supports physical cargo handling.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What fiscal mitigation measure is suggested for EPA-related revenue loss?

options.A: Rely only on foreign borrowing
options.B: Depend only on customs duties permanently
options.C: Eliminate all internal taxes
options.D: Widen the domestic tax base and promote growth to increase income tax and corporate tax revenues

questions.explanation: Domestic tax reform is suggested as a response to revenue loss.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the main transport modes listed in the presentation?

options.A: Air, maritime, rail, road, pipeline, and intermodal transportation
options.B: Only rail and road
options.C: Only air and sea
options.D: Only air, sea, and pipeline

questions.explanation: The presentation lists six major transport modes.

questions.question: What does a negative trade balance mean?

options.A: Exports exceed imports, creating a trade surplus
options.B: The country has no trade agreements
options.C: Imports exceed exports, creating a trade deficit
options.D: The country has no foreign exchange

questions.explanation: A negative trade balance is a deficit.

questions.question: A country depends heavily on imported fuel and medicine. A global shock disrupts supply and the economy suffers. Which trade disadvantage is being illustrated?

options.A: Technology transfer
options.B: Economic dependency and vulnerability
options.C: Breaking down domestic monopolies
options.D: Economies of scale

questions.explanation: Reliance on critical imports can create vulnerability.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.B: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied
options.C: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.D: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: A bank promises to pay the seller if exact documents are presented, but it does not guarantee the actual quality of the goods. Which method is this?

options.A: Consignment
options.B: A letter of credit
options.C: Open account
options.D: A packing list

questions.explanation: Banks under an LC deal with documents, not goods quality.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country with limited oil but strong agricultural capacity exports flowers and tea while importing petroleum products. Which explanation from the presentation best fits this pattern?

options.A: International trade occurs because resources and production capacities are unevenly distributed across countries
options.B: Trade occurs only after customs unions are formed
options.C: Trade occurs only because tariffs are identical everywhere
options.D: Trade occurs only when countries are politically allied

questions.explanation: Unequal resource distribution is one core reason countries trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a telex release or electronic cargo release?

options.A: A tax waiver from customs administration
options.B: A message authorizing duty-free importation
options.C: A certificate of origin transmitted by email
options.D: A message authorizing the carrier to release cargo to a named party without presentation of the original bill of lading

questions.explanation: A telex release substitutes for physical original presentation in relevant cases.

questions.question: Why is a commercial invoice required by customs?

options.A: To authorize export subsidy payment
options.B: To calculate only exchange rates
options.C: To determine the transaction value of imported goods for duties and taxes
options.D: To issue a bill of lading

questions.explanation: Commercial invoices support customs valuation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? What is a consolidator?

options.A: A final consumer of the goods
options.B: A tax authority imposing tariffs
options.C: A customs officer stationed at a port
options.D: A party that groups consignments from different consigners for transport and handles documentation and distribution

questions.explanation: Consolidators combine shipments.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How does international trade increase competition?

options.A: It lowers prices, improves responsiveness to consumer needs, and expands variety
options.B: It guarantees monopoly power for domestic firms
options.C: It reduces choice for consumers in all cases
options.D: It always raises prices

questions.explanation: The presentation emphasizes competition benefits.

questions.question: What is international trade?

options.A: The taxation of domestic production only
options.B: The exchange of goods, services, and capital across national borders
options.C: The movement of people without goods or services
options.D: The exchange of goods only within one country

questions.explanation: The presentation defines international trade as exchange across national borders involving goods, services, and capital.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the presentation? Why is specialized cargo handling important?

options.A: Some goods such as liquids, gases, perishables, or valuables need special handling and suitable modes
options.B: All goods can move exactly the same way
options.C: Only customs officials need to know cargo type
options.D: Handling never affects product quality

questions.explanation: Cargo characteristics affect mode and handling choice.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are official or regulatory documents?

options.A: Documents replacing commercial invoices
options.B: Documents managing risk, ensuring compliance, and supporting customs clearance and legal compliance
options.C: Documents proving sea freight charges only
options.D: Documents used only by sellers and buyers privately

questions.explanation: Official/regulatory documents serve compliance functions.
